questions.question: Indian Railways is under the administrative control of which Ministry?
questions.explanation: Indian Railways functions under the Ministry of Railways, Government of India.

questions.question: The first railway line in India was opened between:
questions.explanation: India's first passenger railway ran between Bori Bunder (Bombay) and Thane, inaugurated on 16 April 1853.

questions.question: The Konkan Railway connects which two cities?
questions.explanation: Konkan Railway runs from Roha (Maharashtra) to Thokur near Mangalore (Karnataka), 756 km.

questions.question: Which gas is used in electric bulbs to prevent the filament from burning?
questions.explanation: Inert gases like Argon (or Nitrogen) are used in electric bulbs to prevent oxidation of the tungsten filament.

questions.question: The SI unit of electric current is:
questions.explanation: The SI unit of electric current is the Ampere (A), named after André-Marie Ampère.

questions.question: Newton's First Law of Motion is also known as:
questions.explanation: Newton's First Law states that a body at rest or uniform motion continues so unless acted on by an external force — hence the Law of Inertia.

questions.question: Photosynthesis takes place in which part of a plant?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis occurs in chloroplasts, mainly found in leaf cells, using sunlight, CO2, and water.

questions.question: The human body's longest bone is the:
questions.explanation: The femur (thigh bone) is the longest and strongest bone in the human body.

questions.question: Which country is known as the 'Land of the Rising Sun'?
questions.explanation: Japan is called the 'Land of the Rising Sun' — its name in Japanese 'Nihon' means 'sun origin'.

questions.question: Mount Everest is located on the border of which two countries?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest (8,849 m) sits on the border between Nepal and the Tibet Autonomous Region of China.

questions.question: The 'Palk Strait' separates India from:
questions.explanation: The Palk Strait is a narrow sea passage between Tamil Nadu (India) and the Mannar district of Sri Lanka.

questions.question: Who is the author of 'Discovery of India'?
questions.explanation: 'The Discovery of India' was written by Jawaharlal Nehru in 1946 while imprisoned at Ahmednagar Fort.

questions.question: The Indian National Congress was founded in:
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress (INC) was founded on 28 December 1885 by Allan Octavian Hume.

questions.question: Who was the first woman Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was the first (and so far only) woman Prime Minister of India, serving 1966–77 and 1980–84.

questions.question: The currency of Japan is:
questions.explanation: The currency of Japan is the Japanese Yen (¥).

questions.question: Which is the largest ocean in the world?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the world's largest ocean, covering more than 165 million km².

questions.question: The Ozone layer is found in which layer of the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: The Ozone layer (15–35 km altitude) is found in the Stratosphere and absorbs harmful UV radiation.

questions.question: Which element has the chemical symbol 'Fe'?
questions.explanation: Fe is the chemical symbol for Iron, derived from the Latin word 'ferrum'.

questions.question: The 'Hornbill Festival' is associated with which state of India?
questions.explanation: The Hornbill Festival is held annually in Nagaland in December, celebrating Naga tribal culture.

questions.question: Which is the highest civilian award in India?
questions.explanation: Bharat Ratna is India's highest civilian honour, instituted in 1954.

questions.question: The Richter scale measures:
questions.explanation: The Richter scale (now largely replaced by the Moment Magnitude Scale) measures the magnitude of earthquakes.

questions.question: Who invented the World Wide Web (WWW)?
questions.explanation: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989 at CERN.

questions.question: Which planet has the most moons in the Solar System?
questions.explanation: Saturn has the most confirmed moons (146 as of 2023), surpassing Jupiter.

questions.question: The first General Elections in independent India were held in:
questions.explanation: India's first General Elections were held from October 1951 to February 1952, won by the Congress party.

questions.question: DNA stands for:
questions.explanation: DNA (Deoxyribonucleic Acid) is the molecule carrying genetic instructions for all living organisms.

questions.question: The headquarters of ISRO is located in:
questions.explanation: ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) headquarters is in Bengaluru (Bangalore), Karnataka.

questions.question: Which bank is the 'Banker of Banks' in India?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is the central bank and acts as the 'Banker of Banks' in India.

questions.question: Panini is associated with which ancient Indian subject?
questions.explanation: Panini (c. 4th century BCE) authored Ashtadhyayi, the foundational text on Sanskrit grammar.

questions.question: Which is the national sport of India?
questions.explanation: Field Hockey is the national sport of India, with India winning 8 Olympic gold medals.

questions.question: CRR stands for:
questions.explanation: CRR (Cash Reserve Ratio) is the percentage of a bank's total deposits that must be maintained as reserves with the RBI.

questions.question: Tsunami is caused by:
questions.explanation: Tsunamis are large sea waves caused by underwater earthquakes, landslides, or volcanic eruptions displacing water.
